questions.question: Why will “He” be given a portion among the great and divide spoil with the strong?
questions.answers: Because He “poured out His soul to death,” and because He bore the sin of many (and will be the means by which transgressors are affected).

questions.question: What will “He” do regarding the spoils?
questions.answers: He will divide spoil ("וְאֶת־עֲצוּמִים יְחַלֵּ֣ק שָׁלָל").

questions.question: What portion will “He” receive?
questions.answers: A portion allotted to Him among the many/great ("אֲחַלֶּק־לֹ֣ו בָרַבִּ֗ים").

questions.question: What did “He” do that led to this outcome?
questions.answers: He poured out His soul to death ("אֲשֶׁר הֶעֱרָה לַמָּוֶת נַפְשֹׁו").

questions.question: What is said about “He” in relation to sin and transgressors?
questions.answers: He bore the sin of many, and He will cause others (the transgressors) to be affected/encounter judgment ("וְאֶת־פֹּשְׁעִ֖ים נִמְנָ֑ה"; "וְלַפֹּשְׁעִ֖ים יַפְגִּיעַ").

questions.question: What relationship is described between “He” and the guilt of many transgressors?
questions.answers: His actions are described as carrying/bearing the guilt/sin of many (“חֵטְא־רַבִּים נָשָׂ֔א”).
